title: A Salute to My Mentor
primary_topics: tribute to Salonga as mentor and guru
law as a brick in the social edifice
sub_topics: Valle Verde Country Club book launch (June 17, 2001)
A Journey of Struggle and Hope (Salonga book)
1956 FEU student strike against retired dean
Salonga's law firm partners (Sedfrey Ordoñez, Pedro Yap, Neptali Gonzales)
Salonga as Senate President with CJ as personal counsel
Liberal Party chief legal counsel role
JBC alleged 'insufficient professional competence' and 'transgressions and sins'

stances.claim: Salonga's most significant teaching is 'not really about the law, as it is about life' — while necessities money can buy (food, shelter, recreation, car, house) are desirable, it is 'far more important to aspire for the things that money cannot buy, like excellence, integrity, honor, dignity and a reverential regard and love for God as the Source of all that is true, good and beautiful.'
stances.rhetorical_move: structured-paired-comparison (necessities vs intangibles) + theistic-source framing of virtue

stances.claim: Salonga is 'a living model of his teachings' — he taught by example, not by words; a dedicated husband, caring father, faithful friend, devoted man of God who 'never compromised his principles and values in exchange for friendship, kinship, relationship, wealth or power' and lives 'an almost ascetic life.'
stances.rhetorical_move: structured-virtue enumeration + chain of relational/material temptations resisted

stances.claim: Their religious differences (Salonga Protestant Cosmopolitan Church leader / CJ Catholic) have not diminished four decades of enriching friendship — 'this is probably because we never discuss what separates us, but only what truly binds us.'
stances.rhetorical_move: interfaith-friendship principle + structural distinction (separates vs binds)

stances.claim: CJ tries to echo Salonga's teaching that 'law cannot be separated from life, that it should be used as a brick in building the social edifice and as a means of fulfilling the deepest aspirations of man' — this is detectable in CJ's decisions, writings, and speeches.

Throughout three trying years of JBC rejection (1992-1995, seven vacancies), Salonga was 'beside me, behind me, and even above me, cheering me up, tirelessly uplifting my sagging spirit and praying for me' — reminding him of Romans 8:28: 'God makes all things work together for the good of those who love Him and are called according to His purpose.'

institutions: Far Eastern University Institute of Law
National Union of Students of the Philippines
events: Valle Verde book launch of 'A Journey of Struggle and Hope' (June 17, 2001)

excellence, integrity, honor, dignity and a reverential regard and love for God
the Source of all that is true, good and beautiful
a brick in building the social edifice
law cannot be separated from life
law… as a means of fulfilling the deepest aspirations of man

we never discuss what separates us, but only what truly binds us
beside me, behind me, and even above me

Nothing is more fulfilling, more exhilarating, than the honor of being counsel to one's mentor and former boss
register_markers: tribute-eulogy register (Salonga still living at this date but soon retired)
fledgling Catholic / devout Protestant interfaith framing
decision_framework_signals: applies necessities-vs-intangibles framing (money-can-buy vs money-cannot-buy) as life-priorities rubric — Salonga's foremost teaching
uses 'law as a brick in the social edifice' metaphor as Salonga-derived jurisprudence (echoes column C11 cyberlibel)
frames interfaith friendship via 'what binds vs what separates' principle
uses Romans 8:28 as career-providence framework during institutional rejection
applies teach-by-example-not-words framing for mentor evaluation
cross_references: page 75 of A Journey of Struggle and Hope (Salonga 2001)
notable_anecdotes: 1956 FEU strike: thousand-strong law students protesting the retired dean, with 36-year-old new dean Salonga (Yale + Harvard, bar topnotcher) calming them in 30 minutes 'with a forceful tremolo' — CJ first encounter with his future mentor

Salonga monitoring CJ's grades constantly when student-leadership absorbed him — calling on him to recite during student-activity peaks — ensuring CJ graduated Latin honors and 'most outstanding student'
1960 bar: CJ sick with rainstorm, wading knee-deep on R. Papa Street from FEU Hospital to UE for exams — wanting to quit, but Salonga 'would not hear of it' and CJ placed 6th
Salonga inviting CJ to join law firm with Ordoñez, Yap, Gonzales as partners — teaching 'integrity, prudence and fairness'
CJ as personal counsel to Salonga during Salonga's Senate presidency + chief legal counsel of the Liberal Party — 'the greatest honor in my career as a practising lawyer'
Seven JBC vacancies 1992-1995, CJ submitted each time, failing each time on 'alleged insufficiency of professional competence and alleged transgressions and sins'
Salonga repeatedly invoking Romans 8:28 during CJ's JBC rejection years
